name: Aditi Kumrawat
title: Student Marks Predictor using Machine Learning
description:
This project is a Machine Learning-based web application developed to predict a student’s final academic performance. The system uses a Linear Regression model to estimate final marks based on key factors such as study hours, attendance percentage, and previous exam scores.

The application is built using Python and utilizes libraries like Scikit-learn for model development, Pandas and NumPy for data handling, and Matplotlib for visualization. A user-friendly interface is created using Streamlit, allowing users to input their data through interactive sliders and instantly receive predicted results.

The project demonstrates the practical implementation of supervised learning and shows how machine learning can be integrated into real-world applications. Additionally, the app is deployed on Streamlit Cloud, making it accessible online.

name: Aditya Tiwari
title: AI Content Detector
description: An application trained on large number of AI-generated and real images and texts, and detects whether the uploaded image/text is AI-generated or real, having an accuracy of 89%.

name: Alankriti dwivedi
title: Ai art generator
description: The AI Art Generator showcases the power of generative AI in transforming imagination into visual reality. It simplifies the art creation process and opens new possibilities for designers, developers, and content creators by combining creativity with machine intelligence.

name: Amay Mishra
title: CivicConnect: AI-Powered Civic Issue Detection and Automated Complaint Generation
description:
CivicConnect is an end-to-end ML/DL project designed to automate urban civic issue reporting by combining computer vision, geolocation intelligence, and text generation. The system takes an uploaded road image as input and uses a custom-trained YOLOv8 deep learning model to classify civic issues into three categories: pothole road, garbage on road, and broken road asphalt. After prediction, the application estimates issue severity from model confidence, captures location through browser geolocation, and performs reverse geocoding to generate a human-readable address.
The final output is not just a class label, but a complete citizen-ready report that includes issue type, confidence score, severity level, timestamp, mapped location, and an auto-generated formal complaint letter for municipal authorities. The solution is implemented as a microservices architecture with separate frontend, backend, and model inference services, making it scalable and deployment-friendly.
This project demonstrates practical use of deep learning for social impact by reducing manual effort, improving reporting speed, and enabling structured communication between citizens and civic bodies. It highlights how AI can move beyond prediction into actionable public-service workflows.

name: AMBAR YADAV
title: STUDENT ACADEMIC TRACKER
description: An AI-powered student achievement forecasting system that predicts final exam marks using machine learning. Built with Streamlit and scikit-learn, it analyzes key academic factors like study hours, attendance, and previous performance to provide accurate grade predictions. Designed for educators, students, and educational institutions to identify at-risk students, estimate grades, and understand academic success patterns through interactive visualizations and real-time predictions

name: Ansh Rathore
title: Aura AI
description:
✨ Aura AI: Precision Neuro-Diagnostic Studio
Aura AI is a professional, clinical-grade diagnostic platform designed to assist in the high-precision detection and localization of brain tumors using axial MRI scans. By leveraging advanced Convolutional Neural Networks (VGG16) and spatial attention modeling, Aura AI provides real-time diagnostic insights with a focus on accuracy, transparency, and high-end user experience.

🚀 Key Features
Clinical-Grade Detection: High-accuracy classification of axial brain MRI slices using a fine-tuned VGG16 transfer-learning architecture.
Grad-CAM Localization: Transparent AI that highlights exactly where the model detects abnormalities, providing visual evidence to support clinical decisions.
Premium SaaS UI: A modern, light-themed diagnostic dashboard built for the "Studio" experience—clean, responsive, and distraction-free.
Real-Time Analytics: Integrated metrics dashboard tracking system accuracy, scan history, and model performance.
Smart Diagnostics: Intelligent heuristics that detect scan orientation and image validity, ensuring the highest data integrity.
🛠️ Tech Stack
Logic Core: Python 3.11
Deep Learning: TensorFlow / Keras (VGG16 Transfer Learning)
Computer Vision: OpenCV (Diagnostic heatmaps & preprocessing)
Frontend: Streamlit (SaaS-grade custom UI)
Visualization: Grad-CAM (Spatial Attention Mapping)
🩺 Use Case
Aura AI is designed for research and educational purposes, providing a bridge between raw machine learning metrics and a professional clinical environment. It transforms complex medical data into actionable, visual insights instantly.

name: Anuj Malviya
title: AI Generated Image Detector
description:
- AI Image Forensics Engine - 

​A web app that spots deepfakes and AI-generated images—and actually explains why they're fake.

​How it works:- 

​Deep Learning Detection: It doesn’t just read metadata. The vision model scans for pixel-level mistakes, weird lighting, and structural glitches that AI generators usually mess up.
​Interactive Chatbot: We solved the machine learning "black box" problem. If an image gets flagged, you can use the built-in chat to ask an LLM exactly what looks manipulated in the photo.
​Smart Backend: Engineered with dynamic API routing. It automatically hunts for live model endpoints so the app doesn't crash if a specific model version gets deprecated.
​Custom SaaS UI: Built on Streamlit, but heavily injected with custom CSS to ditch the default "data dashboard" look in favor of a modern, card-based frontend.

Built with Python, Streamlit, and TensorFlow, the application utilizes a two-stage AI pipeline: a Caffe SSD model for highly accurate face localization, followed by a custom MobileNetV2 neural network for mask classification. description: The goal of this project is to automate the detection of harmful content in social media comments or tweets. By leveraging Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, the model understands the context of sentences to provide accurate classifications.

description: SentiScope AI is a deep learning-powered review intelligence platform built on a fine-tuned BERT Transformer architecture. It performs aspect-level sentiment analysis, fraud detection, and real-time review scoring — all from a single multi-task model trained on 4M+ Amazon reviews with 96.1% accuracy. The platform features an interactive live demo, bulk CSV analysis, and a production-ready REST API for seamless e-commerce integration.

description: This project is a web-based application that uses machine learning to classify and predict diseases based on user-provided symptoms. It analyzes input data to generate possible health conditions along with basic recommendations, promoting early awareness and informed decision-making. The system features an intuitive interface, real-time predictions, and scalable architecture, demonstrating the practical application of AI in healthcare and intelligent diagnostic support system.

The model uses computer vision techniques to process images or live video input. It classifies emotions such as happy, sad, angry, and surprised. It can be further used in areas like mental health monitoring and user experience analysis.

name: Hari Singh
title: SmartStudy AI
description:
SmartStudy AI is a single-page, offline study assistant for college students built entirely in one HTML file with embedded CSS and vanilla JavaScript. It lets a student save a profile, then study across notes, flashcards, exam questions, quizzes, and a visual mind map using hardcoded topic data tailored to their college year.

It also includes a personalized dashboard, daily topic rotation, study streak tracking, recent topics, dark/light mode, PDF-style note printing, and a rule-based chatbot called Buddy. Everything runs in the browser with no backend, no external API, and localStorage handling persistence.

title: EmoTrack AI
description:
EmoTrack AI is a high-performance, real-time facial analytics platform that tracks human expressions and gender with a professional "Vision Pro" aesthetic.

What it Does -
The app captures live video to perform three core AI functions simultaneously:

Intelligent Tracking: Uses precise scanner brackets to detect and track faces.
Emotion Analytics: Classifies moods (Happy, Sad, Angry, etc.) using a deep-learning Xception model.
Temporal Stability: Employs a smoothing engine (30-frame average) to ensure classifications remain rock-solid and flicker-free.

Technologies Used
Core AI: Python, TensorFlow, Keras, and OpenCV.
UI/UX: Streamlit, Streamlit-WebRTC, and custom Vanilla CSS.
Infrastructure: Hosted on GitHub and Streamlit Cloud (Linux environment).

Angry, Disgust, Fear, Happy, Sad, Surprise, Neutral

name: Ishan Singh Tomar
title: Code-Catcher
description:
.

🕸️ Code-Catcher: Deep Learning OCR for Developers

🚀 Overview
Code-Catcher is an AI-powered web application designed to eliminate the tedious process of manually transcribing code from video tutorials, online courses, and presentations. By leveraging state-of-the-art Deep Learning Optical Character Recognition (OCR), Code-Catcher allows developers to upload a screenshot of any code snippet and instantly translates it into clean, copyable, and downloadable text.

💡 The Problem vs. The Solution
The Problem: We all watch coding tutorials where the instructor shares a block of code but leaves out the GitHub link. Pausing the video to type out complex syntax line-by-line breaks momentum and introduces typos.

The Solution: A rapid-prototyping utility where you simply drag and drop a screenshot, and the app's neural network engine extracts the text—preserving vital programming structures like indentation, brackets, and symbols.

🧠 Under the Hood: Models & Tech Stack
This project bridges advanced computer vision with a highly responsive, custom-styled frontend.

Deep Learning / OCR Engine: Powered by EasyOCR (running on the PyTorch backend). Unlike legacy OCR systems (like Tesseract) that struggle with special characters, this model utilizes:

CRAFT (Character Region Awareness for Text Detection): A sophisticated deep-learning algorithm that accurately detects text bounding boxes even in low-resolution video screenshots.

CRNN (Convolutional Recurrent Neural Network): Handles the actual text recognition, heavily trained to recognize complex structures and programming syntax accurately.

Frontend UI/UX: Built with Streamlit using extensively overridden CSS to create a premium, modern dark-mode aesthetic (Inter typography, custom hover states, and smooth loading animations).

Image Processing: Utilizes OpenCV (Headless) and Pillow for robust image handling and matrix transformations before feeding data to the neural network.

✨ Key Features
High-Fidelity Extraction: Tuned to recognize tricky programming symbols { } [ ] ( ) : ; that standard OCRs miss.

Smart Formatting: Includes algorithmic post-processing to intelligently group horizontal text blocks and estimate spatial indentation.

Premium Dark Mode Interface: A distraction-free, developer-focused UI with an intuitive drag-and-drop dropzone.

One-Click Actions: Features a native st.code block for instant copy-to-clipboard functionality, plus a dedicated Download (.py) button to export the code directly to your local machine.

⚙️ Live Deployment
This application is containerized and deployed seamlessly on Streamlit Community Cloud, requiring zero local setup for end-users to enjoy lightning-fast code extraction.

name: Ishika Sethiya
title: AI Waste Classification System
description:
AI Waste Classification System ♻️
A complete end-to-end Deep Learning system that classifies waste into Biodegradable and Recyclable categories using CNNs and Transfer Learning.

🚀 Features
Dual Model Support: Custom CNN and MobileNetV2 (Transfer Learning).
Interactive UI: Streamlit dashboard for image upload and live webcam input.
Smart Suggestions: Provides disposal advice for each category.
Training Analytics: Visualizes Accuracy/Loss and performance metrics.
Real-time Dashboard: Tracks classification statistics.
📁 Project Structure
dataset/: Organizes images for training and validation.
models/: Stores trained model files (.h5) and evaluation plots.
src/: Core logic for model building, preprocessing, and utilities.
app.py: The Streamlit web application.
train.py: Script to train and compare models.
dataset_setup.py: Utility to prepare folder structure and data.
🛠️ Getting Started
1. Installation
Ensure you have Python installed. Install dependencies:

pip install -r requirements.txt
2. Dataset Preparation
Run the setup script to create the folders:

python dataset_setup.py
Important: Download the Waste Classification Dataset from Kaggle and place the images in dataset/train/ and dataset/val/ under the respective class folders.

3. Training
Train the models using:

python train.py
This will save custom_cnn.h5 and transfer_learning.h5 in the models/ folder.

4. Running the Dashboard
Launch the Streamlit app:

streamlit run app.py
🧠 Model Details
Custom CNN: 3 Convolutional layers with MaxPooling and Dropout.
Transfer Learning: MobileNetV2 base with custom Dense layers (Frozen weights for faster training).
💡 Smart Suggestions
Biodegradable → Compost (Green)
Recyclable → Recycle bin (Blue)
Developed as a production-ready AI Waste Classification project.

By leveraging high-resolution satellite imagery from NASA (Landsat-8/9) and ESA (Sentinel-2) via the Microsoft Planetary Computer, this tool provides a multi-temporal analysis of Land Surface Temperature (LST) and Vegetation Density (NDVI). The project features a Spatial-Aware Random Forest model that allows urban planners to simulate "what-if" scenarios, predicting the cooling impact of green-cover restoration to drive climate-resilient municipal policy.

name: Lav Bhatia
title: Heart Desease Prediction App
description:
This project is a website that uses machine learning to figure out if someone is likely to have heart disease. It looks at things like how old the person's if they are a man or a woman what kind of chest pain they have their blood pressure and other health things. The website uses something called the Support Vector Machine algorithm to make predictions. This algorithm is really good at looking at lots of information and making decisions.

The website lets people put in their health information like their age, sex and blood pressure. It also asks about things like chest pain and cholesterol levels. The website then looks at all this information. Uses it to make a prediction. It does this by using the steps it used when it was learning from other peoples health information.

The part of the website that people do not see is built using something called Flask. This makes the website small and easy to use. When someone puts in their health information the website uses a tool to make a prediction right away. It then tells the person if they are likely to have heart disease or not.

When someone puts in their information the website says one of two things:

* No Heart Disease Detected ✅

* Risk of Heart Disease ⚠️

This project shows how machine learning can be used in real life to help people. It focuses on making predictions letting people use the website and putting all the pieces together to make something that works. The machine learning model is used to predict heart disease and the project is, about making this model work well. The heart disease prediction is the goal of the project.

name: Manasvi sohani
title: Smart Expense Tracker
description: Smart Expense Tracker with AI is an intelligent application that automates expense management using Deep Learning and Natural Language Processing (NLP). The system allows users to enter expenses in text form (e.g., “Pizza ₹200”) and automatically classifies them into categories such as Food, Travel, Shopping, and Bills. It uses an LSTM-based model with an embedding layer to understand and process text data effectively. The application stores all transactions in a database and provides a user-friendly dashboard to visualize spending patterns through graphs and charts. This helps users analyze their financial habits and manage budgets efficiently. The project reduces manual effort, improves accuracy in categorization, and demonstrates real-world implementation of AI in personal finance. Future enhancements include receipt scanning using OCR, voice input support, and mobile app integration for better accessibility and usability.

name: MANSI JAIN
title: Resume Analyzer
description:
Project Description: Resume Analyzer (AI-Powered)

The Resume Analyzer is an intelligent web-based application designed to evaluate and enhance resumes using Machine Learning (ML) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques. The system allows users to upload resumes in PDF or DOCX format or input text manually, and then performs a detailed analysis to assess resume quality and effectiveness.

The application uses advanced NLP methods to extract key skills from the resume and compares them against industry-relevant requirements. It employs a TF-IDF vectorization technique combined with Logistic Regression to classify resumes into categories such as Good, Average, or Poor, simulating the behavior of real-world Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS).

In addition to scoring, the system provides actionable suggestions to improve resume content, including grammar corrections, better use of action verbs, formatting improvements, and structure optimization. A key feature of the application is its ability to match resumes with job descriptions by calculating similarity scores using cosine similarity, helping users understand how well their resume aligns with a specific job role.

The project also identifies missing skills and highlights areas that need improvement to increase ATS compatibility. Furthermore, users can download a comprehensive PDF report containing the complete analysis and recommendations.

Built using Python and Flask for the backend, along with scikit-learn and NLTK for ML/NLP processing, and standard web technologies for the frontend, this project demonstrates a practical implementation of AI in career development tools.

name: Mansi Wahane
title: laptop-price-prediction-project
description:
The Laptop Price Prediction System is a machine learning-based project developed to estimate the price of a laptop based on its specifications. With the rapid growth of the electronics market, it has become difficult for users to determine the fair price of laptops with varying configurations. This project aims to solve this problem by building a predictive model that analyzes key features such as processor type, RAM, storage, GPU, screen size, and brand.

The system uses supervised machine learning algorithms to train a model on a dataset of laptops and their corresponding prices. After training, the model can predict the price of a new laptop when the user inputs its specifications. This helps customers make informed purchasing decisions and also assists sellers in pricing their products competitively.

The system also gives a confidence score to show how well the skills match those jobs. It makes the hiring process simpler and more efficient.

title: SignatureAuth

name: Nandani Rathore
title: Posture and eye motion detector
description:
AI-Based Eye Strain & Posture Monitoring System is a real-time computer vision application designed to promote healthy screen usage habits. The system uses a webcam to continuously monitor the user’s eye activity, posture, and distance from the screen. By applying image processing techniques through OpenCV and facial and body landmark detection using MediaPipe, it identifies signs of eye fatigue, improper posture, and unsafe viewing distance.
When such conditions are detected, the system provides immediate on-screen alerts, such as reminders to maintain proper posture, keep a safe distance from the screen, and take breaks to reduce eye strain. This solution is particularly useful for students and professionals who spend extended periods working on digital devices, helping to improve their overall health and productivity.

name: niharika soni
title: sign language translator
description:
This project is a web-based Sign Language Translator designed to improve communication between hearing and hearing-impaired individuals. The application translates sign language gestures into readable text, making interactions more accessible and inclusive. It is built using modern web technologies, ensuring a simple, responsive, and user-friendly interface.

The system leverages computer vision and machine learning techniques to detect and interpret hand gestures accurately. By processing visual inputs, it converts gestures into meaningful language in real time. This helps users communicate effectively in situations where sign language interpreters are not available.

The project highlights the practical use of artificial intelligence in assistive technology. It focuses on usability, accessibility, and real-world impact. Overall, the Sign Language Translator demonstrates how technology can bridge communication gaps and support inclusivity, making it a valuable solution for enhancing interaction and understanding in diverse environments.

name: Nivedika Gour
title: Soulreplica
description:
Project: AI Personal Companion (Soul Replica)
Overview

An AI-powered personal assistant that acts as a digital twin, combining journaling, habit tracking, expense management, and conversational AI to provide personalized insights and guidance.

Core Idea
A system that:

Stores long-term personal thoughts, conversations, and habits
Learns user behavior over time
Provides contextual advice, reminders, and suggestions

Key Features
Conversational AI (Text + Voice):
Chat and speak with the assistant for advice, reflection, and idea generation
Persistent Memory:
Stores thoughts, stories, and personal quotes; retrieves them contextually
Voice Assistant:

Habit Tracking:
Track routines with smart feedback and motivational alerts
Expense Tracking:
Log income/expenses, set budgets, receive spending insights
Personalized Insights:

Quotes & Reminders:

name: Prabhat Choudhary
title: Taaranaa Mood Scanner: Passive Emotion Monitoring (CNN & FER-2013)
description: Taaranaa Mood Scanner is a deployed web application focused on holistic wellness through passive emotion monitoring. The project utilizes Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transfer Learning to perform real-time Image Processing and Facial Emotion Recognition. It leverages the DeepFace framework, running on models trained via the Kaggle FER-2013 dataset. Technically, the app processes user-uploaded images using OpenCV to isolate facial features, passes the pixel arrays through the CNN to classify emotional states (e.g., Happy, Sad, Neutral), and returns the prediction through a Python backend. The entire ML pipeline is successfully deployed as a live cloud web service.

name: Prachi Patidar
title: AI-Based Waste Segregation and Recycling Suggestion System
description:
This project is a web-based application that classifies waste into different categories such as Organic, Plastic, Glass, Paper, and Metal using intelligent image analysis. Users can upload an image of waste, and the system predicts its category and provides appropriate recycling suggestions.

The system was initially designed using deep learning concepts and later optimized with a lightweight hybrid approach to ensure faster and more stable performance during deployment. It focuses on real-time usability, accuracy for common waste types, and practical recycling guidance.

The application aims to promote environmental awareness by helping users correctly segregate waste and understand proper disposal methods.

name: Prachi Patidar
title: NutriVision AI – Food Calorie Estimator System
description:
NutriVision AI is a smart system that uses Artificial Intelligence and Deep Learning to estimate the calorie content of food from images. Users can upload or capture a food image, and the system detects and classifies the food items using computer vision models like CNN or YOLO.

After identifying the food, it maps the items to a nutrition dataset to calculate approximate calorie values. This project helps users track their diet and make healthier food choices. It can be further extended with features like portion size estimation and multi-food detection.

Objective:
To develop an AI-based solution for automatic food recognition and calorie estimation.

description:
This project focuses on building a Sentiment Analysis System that automatically classifies text into positive, negative, or neutral sentiment based on the words and context used in the input.

The system takes textual data (such as product reviews, comments, or social media posts) and processes it using Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques. It analyzes the presence of emotionally significant words and patterns to determine the overall sentiment of the text.

title: Safe Lancer: AI-Powered Fraud Detection & Secure Freelancing Platform
description:
Safe Lancer is a machine learning–based web application designed to enhance security and trust in freelancing platforms. The system focuses on detecting fraudulent activities, suspicious users, and unsafe transactions using intelligent algorithms.
The platform leverages machine learning models to analyze user behavior, transaction patterns, and activity data to identify potential risks. By applying predictive analytics, the system can classify users or actions as safe or suspicious, helping prevent scams and improving reliability in freelance ecosystems.

Coursel is an interactive web application built using Streamlit that allows users to explore and browse curated courses in a visually engaging carousel format. It provides a modern, user-friendly interface where each course is presented with images, titles, and descriptions, enabling seamless navigation and quick discovery of learning content. Designed for simplicity and responsiveness, Coursel enhances the way users interact with educational resources by combining clean design with dynamic presentation

name: Rajn kumawat
title: Cardio-AI
description: CARDIO AI is a machine learning-based web application that predicts the risk of heart disease using patient health data. It takes inputs like age, blood pressure, cholesterol, and heart rate, and provides a risk score along with alert levels (Low, Medium, High). The system uses a trained Random Forest model and includes rule-based logic for detecting critical conditions. It is built with a Flask backend and an interactive frontend, enabling real-time prediction and early warning support.

name: Ram Nagar
title: Chatbot using pretrained model
description: An end-to-end conversational AI web application integrating Microsoft's DialoGPT model. Built with FastAPI for robust backend routing and Streamlit for rapid UI deployment, featuring conversation memory and dynamic text generation

name: Rishabh Tirole
title: DeepVision
description: Upload an image to instantly detect humans, animals, vegetables, and everyday objects using advanced Deep Learning.

name: Sahil Ali
title: Agri-Sanchar
description: This project focuses on the design and development of an AI-enabled, multilingual mobile advisory system for small and marginal farmers. The system provides location-specific crop advisories, pest and disease detection, weather updates, and market insights to help farmers make informed decisions and improve agricultural productivity.

name: Sanatan Singh Rajawat
title: Vehicle detection
description: The main purpose of this project is to use artificial intelligence to automatically detect, locate, and highlight vehicles (like cars, trucks, and buses) inside images uploaded by the user.

name: Sanvi Nahar
title: Smart Movie Recommender System
description:
This project is a web-based movie recommendation system that suggests similar movies based on user input. It uses a content-based filtering approach, analyzing movie descriptions and genres to compute similarity between films. The system is built using Python (Flask) for the backend and a responsive HTML/CSS/JavaScript interface for the frontend.

Users can enter a movie title and instantly receive a list of recommended movies along with posters and similarity scores. The application integrates the OMDB API to fetch real-time movie posters, enhancing the user experience. To ensure smooth performance, the model is optimized using TF-IDF vectorization and cosine similarity, with lazy loading to improve speed on deployment platforms like Render.

The project demonstrates practical implementation of machine learning concepts, REST API development, and full-stack deployment, making it suitable for real-world applications and portfolio showcase.

name: Sarthak Siddharth Sahare
title: Ballistics Encyclopedia
description: Educational firearm ballistics encyclopedia built with Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, Fuse.js, and Recharts.

name: Shailendra Charan
title: Academic Content Generator
description: Academic Content Generator is a Python web app that creates structured academic research paper drafts from any topic. It generates sections like Title, Abstract, Introduction, Literature Review, Methodology, Results and Discussion, and Conclusion using either an offline generator or the OpenAI API.

name: Sudiksha Sirohi
title: FakeGuard AI
description: FakeGuard AI is a deep learning-based Fake News Detection System that automatically classifies news articles as Fake or Real using an LSTM neural network trained on 44,000+ labeled articles. The model uses an NLP preprocessing pipeline — including text cleaning, tokenization, and sequence padding — before passing data through Embedding, LSTM, and Dense layers to produce a confidence-scored prediction. It achieves approximately 94–96% accuracy on the test dataset using Binary Cross-Entropy loss and the Adam optimizer.

name: Surabhi Solanki
title: Covid-19 Research Assistant AI
description:
This platform is an advanced AI research ecosystem built on PyTorch and Hugging Face Transformers, utilizing SBERT semantic embeddings to perform high-precision search across thousands of scholarly documents stored in ChromaDB. It integrates Google Gemini 1.5 as the core LLM for RAG-powered paper drafting and summarization, while leveraging scispaCy for specialized medical named entity recognition. The final system is deployed as a high-performance Streamlit application, providing researchers with a state-of-the-art deep learning pipeline for rapid scientific discovery. 
The platform also generates research papers related to COVID-19

This is a rough project and still in development. More functionalities and UI will be added later

name: suryakant chaturvedi
title: antigravity-jarvis
description: antigravity-jarvis

name: TANISH GUPTA
title: Research-Paper-Content-Generator
description:
Academic Writing Workspace,
Research Paper Content Generator,
Generate structured academic research paper content from a topic using a formal, research-oriented writing style.

Generated sections
Title, Abstract, Introduction, Literature Review, Methodology, Results & Discussion, and Conclusion.

name: Tanisha Jain
title: NIRVANA.ai – AI-Powered System Architecture Visualization Platform
description:
NIRVANA.ai is an AI-driven platform that converts natural language system design prompts into structured architecture visualizations and quantitative system metrics. The platform processes user input through an LLM-based pipeline using Groq and LangChain to generate structured JSON outputs representing system components, architecture diagrams, and performance metrics.

The backend parses and validates these outputs to extract quantitative data such as load distribution, bottlenecks, and architectural constraints. The frontend dynamically renders interactive architecture diagrams, system maps, and quantitative performance metrics to help developers analyze and optimize system designs.

This tool helps engineers, students, and architects quickly prototype scalable system architectures, visualize infrastructure components, and understand system behavior without manually designing complex diagrams.

name: Tanisha Kariya
title: Smart Resume Screener
description:
“This project is a Smart Resume Screening System that uses Machine Learning to automatically analyze resumes and check how well they match a job role.”

1> How it Works- “First, the system takes the resume and converts it into text. Then it cleans the text and extracts important information like skills. After that, we use a Machine Learning technique called TF-IDF to convert the text into numbers. Finally, a Logistic Regression model analyzes it and gives a match score and recommendation.”

2> Output Explanation- “The system shows: Match percentage (like 75%) Skills found in the resume Final suggestion: Suitable or needs improvement”

3> Technologies Used- “We used Python, Flask for backend, and Machine Learning techniques like TF-IDF and Logistic Regression.”

4> Real-Life Use- “This system can help HR teams shortlist candidates faster and reduce manual work.”

name: Tanvi Shrivastava
title: Diabetes-predictor
description: Smart Diabetes Predictor is a machine learning-based mini project developed to predict whether a person is diabetic or non-diabetic using important medical parameters such as pregnancies, glucose level, blood pressure, skin thickness, insulin, BMI, diabetes pedigree function, and age. The project includes data preprocessing, missing value handling, feature scaling, feature selection, model training, and performance evaluation using multiple supervised learning algorithms like Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine. The model  provides real-time prediction, probability score, risk level, and basic result explanation through an easy-to-use interface.

name: Vanshika patel
title: Smart URl and scam detector
description: Smart URL and Scam Detection is a machine learning-based project that identifies and classifies suspicious or harmful URLs. It analyzes various features of URLs to detect phishing, fraud, and scam-related activities. The system helps enhance online security by preventing users from accessing unsafe websites.

name: Vedika Butani
title: News Article Detector
description: News Detection is a machine learning-based project that classifies news articles as real or fake using Natural Language Processing techniques. It analyses text data and predicts authenticity through a trained model.

name: Vinay Pratap Singh Thakur
title: Laptop AI Price Predictor
description: AI-powered laptop price estimation with quality scoring & recommendations

name: Vishwajeet Thakur
title: Jarvis
description: AntiGravity Jarvis is a modern AI assistant frontend inspired by futuristic virtual assistants like Jarvis. Built with React and Vite, it provides a responsive and visually engaging interface for interacting with AI systems, designed to be extended with voice, automation, and intelligent backend capabilities.

name: Yash sikarwar
title: Resume-screener
description: Machine learning model for a resume screener that classifies and ranks candidates based on job relevance

name: yuvraj singh pawar
title: Movie Recommendation System
description: A full-stack movie recommendation system that combines TF-IDF content-based filtering with real-time movie data from the OMDB API. Built with a FastAPI backend and a Streamlit frontend, CineMatch helps you discover your next favorite movie through intelligent, AI-driven similarity analysis.
